gaps: Local-dev Docker stack and migrations are out of scope — use the official supabase CLI for `supabase start`, `supabase db push`, `supabase gen types`
Realtime WebSocket subscribe is out of scope (doesn't fit one-shot CLI shape)
Auth user PII is never synced to the local SQLite store; live queries only

novel_features.name: Cross-project secret-name audit
novel_features.description: Find every project (across orgs) holding a secret with a given name, plus when each was last synced.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a LEFT JOIN over locally-synced secret_names + projects + organizations tables; no Management API endpoint answers this in one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs cross-project security posture answers ('which clients have STRIPE_KEY', 'is this secret name leaked anywhere'). The supabase-community MCP cannot answer this without per-project iteration.

novel_features.name: Function inventory rollup
novel_features.description: Per-project, per-org rollup of every edge function with slug, version, status, and last-deployed timestamp.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates locally-synced project_functions rows by project_ref and org_id; live API only lists functions per project.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during deploy review ('which projects deployed stripe-webhook?') or stale-function audits ('which functions haven't redeployed in 90 days?').

novel_features.name: Branch-drift sweep
novel_features.description: List preview branches older than N days that haven't been merged or deleted, grouped by parent project.
novel_features.rationale: Age filter over locally-synced project_branches; live Mgmt API has no stale-branch filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during weekly cleanup to prevent preview-branch sprawl that costs DB resources.

novel_features.name: Auth user lookup with context
novel_features.description: Look up an Auth user by email and optionally join their row from a user-named PostgREST context table on user_id.
novel_features.rationale: Pairs Auth Admin /admin/users?email= with optional PostgREST select on a user table; neither surface exists in the official CLI or in supabase-community MCP.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use during support-ticket triage to see the user plus their domain row in one envelope instead of three dashboard clicks.

novel_features.name: RLS-aware schema introspection
novel_features.description: Fetch the per-project PostgREST OpenAPI from the Management API and list tables, columns, types, and detected indexes for typed query planning.
novel_features.rationale: Uses Management API GET /v1/projects/{ref}/api/rest, the documented replacement for the anon-key /rest/v1/ OpenAPI fetch being removed April 2026.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before authoring a typed query so an agent knows the table's column types and constraints without trial-and-error.

novel_features.name: Project estate rollup
novel_features.description: One-row-per-project rollup of function count, branch count, api-key count, secret-name count, and last-synced-at.
novel_features.rationale: LEFT JOIN across four locally-synced entity tables; no live endpoint answers this aggregation. Named `estate` to avoid collision with the spec-derived `projects health` (Management /health/services).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for the Monday morning estate review; one screen replaces 12 dashboard tabs.

novel_features.name: Storage bucket usage rollup
novel_features.description: For each bucket, list objects and aggregate file count, total bytes, and largest object.
novel_features.rationale: Pages the real Storage list endpoint and aggregates; surfaces 'is my free tier full' without writing a script.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to answer 'how close are we to the storage ceiling' or 'which bucket is biggest'.

novel_features.name: Cross-project recent signups
novel_features.description: Iterate synced projects, call Auth Admin per project, aggregate users created within the window into one table.
novel_features.rationale: Fan-out over per-project Auth Admin calls + cross-project aggregation; no single competitor surfaces this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for weekly user-growth review across an entire org portfolio.

narrative.headline: Every Supabase runtime endpoint — PostgREST CRUD, Auth Admin, Storage objects, Edge Function invoke — plus the full Management API and a local SQLite cache of orgs, projects, functions, branches, and secret names.
narrative.value_prop: The official supabase CLI is local-dev tooling (Docker, migrations, types). This CLI is the runtime API surface it omits, with `--json --select --dry-run` consistency across every command. The local store enables cross-project queries (`secrets where-name STRIPE_KEY`, `branches drift --older-than 7d`, `functions inventory --org acme`) that no single live API call answers.
narrative.auth_narrative: Supabase has three credential types across two API hosts. The Management API at api.supabase.com uses a Personal Access Token (`SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN`, header `Authorization: Bearer <PAT>`). Project APIs at `<ref>.supabase.co` use the `apikey:` header — set `SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` (or legacy `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`) for RLS-respecting calls, or `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (or new `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY`) for server-side calls that bypass RLS and unlock Auth Admin endpoints. Also set `SUPABASE_URL=https://<ref>.supabase.co`. Edge Functions gotcha: keys go in `apikey:`, never `Authorization: Bearer`.

quickstart.comment: Verify both Management and project credentials are wired and reachable.
List your Supabase projects via the Management API (requires SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN).
Populate the local SQLite store with orgs/projects/functions/branches/secrets for cross-project queries.
Cross-project audit: every project holding the named secret.
Fetch the per-project PostgREST schema via Management API (replacement for the anon-key path being removed April 2026).

troubleshoots.symptom: 401 Unauthorized on Auth Admin or RLS-bypassing PostgREST calls
troubleshoots.fix: These endpoints require the service_role key. Set `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (or new `SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY`); the publishable/anon key is RLS-only.
Keys go in the `apikey:` header, not `Authorization: Bearer`. The CLI sets this correctly by default; check that you haven't overridden the auth header via --header.
Management API needs a Personal Access Token (sbp_...), not a project key. Generate one at supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens and `export SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN=...`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results from `pgrst select` despite knowing rows exist
troubleshoots.fix: Row-Level Security is filtering them out. Either use the service_role key (bypasses RLS) or sign in as a user whose JWT passes the table's RLS policy.
Sync at least once: `supabase-pp-cli sync` populates the local store. Drift compares synced rows to filter conditions; an empty store has nothing to compare.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent needs to interact with Supabase at runtime — query user tables, manage Auth users, upload Storage objects, invoke Edge Functions, sweep Management API entities across orgs — with consistent `--json --select --dry-run` ergonomics. For local Docker + migration workflows (`supabase start`, `supabase db push`, `supabase gen types`), use the official supabase CLI; this CLI focuses on what the official one explicitly omits.

recipes.title: Cross-project secret audit
recipes.command: supabase-pp-cli sync && supabase-pp-cli secrets where-name STRIPE_KEY --json
recipes.explanation: Sync the local store, then list every project across every org holding a secret named STRIPE_KEY — the Monday-morning security sweep in one shell call.

recipes.title: Support ticket triage
recipes.explanation: Look up an Auth user by email; --select narrows the payload to identity fields. Add --context-table <table> --context-key <col> to join a domain row.

recipes.title: Stale preview branches
recipes.explanation: Surface preview branches older than 14 days that have not been merged or deleted, grouped by parent project — the Tuesday cleanup target list.

recipes.title: Storage ceiling check
recipes.explanation: List every bucket over 100MB; pipe through jq to filter on byte thresholds.

recipes.title: PostgREST schema before query
recipes.explanation: Get just the column types of the orders table from the Management-API-routed schema endpoint, so an agent can author a typed PostgREST query without trial-and-error.
